summary: Go-based testing and orchestration framework with modular internal packages for assertions, schema validation, test running, MCP handling, and reporting, developed through automated agent-driven sessions.
patterns: agent-based task orchestration with numbered sessions
extensive test coverage with extra test files per module
concerns: large number of agent session directories (GOrchestra/glm-agents/) suggesting heavy automated code generation
test files appear to outnumber source files potentially indicating test sprawl
truncated directory names in agent paths suggest filesystem or naming constraints
staged and untracked files indicate incomplete merge or work-in-progress
suggestions: consolidate or archive completed glm-agent session directories to reduce repo noise
review generated test files for duplication and merge valuable tests into main test suites
add integration tests to validate runner, schema, and reporter interactions end-to-end
document the agent orchestration workflow and session numbering convention
